title: The Black Bordered Letter
content:
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ SO ï؟½eï؟½s dead in London,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ answered to the call,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ trotted through the Long Street,
آ آ آ آ With ï؟½earse anï؟½ plumes anï؟½ all?
آ آ آ آ We was village boys anï؟½ brothers,
آ آ آ آ We was warm as we could be,
آ آ آ آ In the milk-walk anï؟½ the fried fish,
آ آ آ آ Up in London, ï؟½im anï؟½ me.

آ آ آ آ We was warm,
آ آ آ آ We was warm,
آ آ آ آ As we ï؟½ad always been;
آ آ آ آ We never ï؟½ad a dry word
آ آ آ آ Till she come between.

آ آ آ آ I lived round Windsor Terrace,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ ï؟½im across the wye,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ when I sailed a emigrant
آ آ آ آ We never said good-bye!
آ آ آ آ He wos better than a brother,
آ آ آ آ Wot you Bushmen call a mate.
آ آ آ آ (Did he reach the rylwye stytion,
آ آ آ آ As they told me, just too late!)

آ آ آ آ We was warm,
آ آ آ آ We was warm,
آ آ آ آ As pals was ever seen;
آ آ آ آ We never ï؟½ad a dry word
آ آ آ آ Till she come between.

آ آ آ آ I meant to go back ï؟½ome again,
آ آ آ آ I meant to write to-night;
آ آ آ آ I meant to write by every mail,
آ آ آ آ But I thought ï؟½e oughter write.
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ now ï؟½eï؟½s left North London,
آ آ آ آ For a better place, perhaps,
آ آ آ آ Sheï؟½s flauntinï؟½ in ï؟½er widder weeds,
آ آ آ آ With eyes on other chaps.

آ آ آ آ We was warm,
آ آ آ آ We was warm,
آ آ آ آ As we ï؟½ad always been;
آ آ آ آ We never ï؟½ad a dry word
آ آ آ آ Till she come between.

آ آ آ آ Oh! tongues is bad in wimmin,
آ آ آ آ When wimminï؟½s tongues is bad!
آ آ آ آ For theyï؟½ll part men anï؟½ brothers
آ آ آ آ World oceans wide, my lad!
آ آ آ آ There was seven years between us,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ fifteen thousand mile,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ now thereï؟½s death anï؟½ sorrer
آ آ آ آ For ever anï؟½ awhile.

آ آ آ آ We was warm,
آ آ آ آ We was warm,
آ آ آ آ As two was ever seen;
آ آ آ آ We never ï؟½ad a dry word
آ آ آ آ Till she come between.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Henry Lawson represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Henry Lawson's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
